checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss start=2013-09-28 end=2014-09-26 value=206103000; NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities start=2013-09-28 end=2014-09-26 value=361547000
evidence.location: FY2014 10-K income statement and cash flow statement

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss start=2012-09-29 end=2013-09-27 value=189271000; NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities start=2012-09-29 end=2013-09-27 value=276501000
evidence.location: FY2013 10-K income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit start=2013-09-28 end=2014-09-26 value=890000000; Revenues start=2013-09-28 end=2014-09-26 value=960176000 (92.7% margin)
InventoryNet end=2014-09-26 value=8536000; then end=2014-12-26 value=28291000
evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet transition FY2014-FY2015, likely acquisition-related inventory addition

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Revenues start=2013-12-28 end=2014-03-28 value=278592000 vs Revenues start=2014-03-29 end=2014-06-27 value=223350000 (25% swing); NetIncomeLoss same periods 75868000 vs 39779000 (proportional swing)
evidence.location: 10-Q quarterly income statements FY2014 Q2 vs Q3

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2013-09-27 value=97460000 (allowance coverage fell to ~0.53% of AR vs ~4.0% in FY2011)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2011-11-23 followed by 10-K/A filed 2011-11-30 (within one week)
8-K/A filed 2011-06-28 and 8-K/A filed 2011-12-05, indicating multiple amendment filings

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series span FY2008 (2007-09-29) through Q3 FY2015 (2015-06-26) across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet, and cash flow statement items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Allowance for doubtful accounts / bad debt reserve estimation - AR grew ~124% (43,495K to 97,460K) between FY2012 and FY2013 while revenue declined slightly (933,014K to 909,674K) and the allowance for doubtful accounts fell from 2,466K (FY2011) to 514K (FY2013), reducing reserve coverage from ~4.0% of AR to ~0.53%. This pattern is consistent with an inadequately reserved, growing receivables balance that could overstate net receivables and net income if collectability risk is understated.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2013-09-27 value=97460000 vs end=2012-09-28 value=43495000
Revenues start=2012-09-29 end=2013-09-27 value=909674000 (down from 933014000 in FY2012)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase price allocation in a business combination (goodwill and intangible assets jumped sharply in Q1 FY2015, coincident with likely acquisition activity) could bias allocation toward goodwill (non-amortizing) versus definite-lived intangibles, deferring expense recognition and inflating near-term reported earnings relative to the economic cost of the acquired assets.

rationale_evidence.quote: Goodwill end=2014-12-26 value=315791000 vs end=2014-09-26 value=277574000 (14% jump in one quarter)
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end=2014-12-26 value=108220000 vs end=2014-09-26 value=63700000 (70% jump in one quarter)
Revenues start=2014-09-27 end=2014-12-26 value=234238000, roughly flat versus prior-year quarter Revenues start=2013-09-28 end=2013-12-27 value=231277000

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Aggressive capitalization of costs (e.g., headquarters construction and related capex) - gross PP&E grew from 143,468K (2010-09-24) to 590,301K (2015-06-26), a more than 4x increase, while revenue remained roughly flat (~920M-960M annually) over the same period, raising the possibility that operating expenditures were capitalized rather than expensed, understating current period operating expenses and overstating operating income.

rationale_evidence.quote: PropertyPlantAndEquipmentGross end=2010-09-24 value=143468000 vs end=2015-06-26 value=590301000
Revenues start=2009-09-26 end=2010-09-24 value=922713000 vs Revenues start=2013-09-28 end=2014-09-26 value=960176000 (revenue nearly flat over 4-5 years while PP&E quadrupled)
rationale_evidence.location: FY2014 10-K income statement, comparative revenue trend

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew ~124% (43,495K to 97,460K) from FY2012 to FY2013 while revenue declined slightly and allowance for doubtful accounts fell from 2,466K to 514K, sharply reducing reserve coverage

Goodwill and intangible assets jumped sharply (Goodwill +14%, Intangibles +70%) in a single quarter (Q1 FY2015) while revenue was roughly flat, consistent with acquisition-related purchase price allocation risk
Gross PP&E grew over 4x (143,468K to 590,301K) between FY2010 and mid-FY2015 while revenue remained essentially flat, raising capitalization-policy risk
Minor filing chronology irregularities including a 10-K/A filed one week after the original FY2011 10-K and multiple 8-K/A amendments
